---
title: React UI rules
scope: project
---

<!-- Generated by `harness rules bootstrap`. Edit to tune; re-run to regenerate. -->

- Keep components and Hooks pure: given the same props, state, and context they MUST return the same JSX. NEVER perform side effects, I/O, or mutation during render — rendering may run multiple times (Strict Mode double-invokes).
- NEVER mutate props, state, context, or any value already passed to a Hook or used in JSX. Treat each render's values as an immutable snapshot; build new objects/arrays and set state via updater functions.
- Call Hooks ONLY at the top level of a component or custom Hook — never inside conditions, loops, nested functions, or after an early return. Keep `eslint-plugin-react-hooks` enabled and never suppress its dependency-array warnings by hand.
- Derive, don't store: compute values from existing props/state during render instead of mirroring them into `useState` + an Effect. Reserve `useMemo`/`useCallback` for computations or referential stability you have actually measured as needed.
- Use `useEffect` ONLY to synchronize with external systems (subscriptions, non-React widgets, data fetch with cleanup to avoid race conditions). NEVER use Effects for data transforms, user-event logic (put it in the handler), or chains of state updates that trigger each other.
- Reset a child's state by passing a changed `key`, not by clearing it in an Effect.
- Give every list item a stable key derived from data (e.g. an id); NEVER use the array index as key for lists that can reorder, filter, or grow.
- Mark interactive/stateful components with `"use client"`; keep them small and leaf-level, and keep data fetching, secrets, and heavy logic in Server Components / server code — never ship secrets to a Client Component.
- Use semantic HTML (`<button>`, `<a>`, `<nav>`, `<label>`, headings) instead of `<div onClick>`; native elements provide role, keyboard operability, and focus for free. Reach for `aria-*` only to express what HTML cannot (React passes `aria-*` and `data-*` through unchanged).
- Associate every form control with a `<label>` via `htmlFor`/`id` (JSX uses `htmlFor` and `className`, not `for`/`class`); give meaningful `alt` text to images and accessible names to icon-only controls.
- Ensure all interactive UI is keyboard-operable with a visible focus indicator, and move focus deliberately on route changes, dialog open/close, and async content reveals.

<!-- sources fetched at generation: https://react.dev/reference/rules, https://react.dev/reference/rules/rules-of-hooks, https://react.dev/reference/rules/components-and-hooks-must-be-pure, https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect, https://react.dev/learn/rendering-lists -->
